title: LCO mechanism smoke
domain: high-voltage lithium cobalt oxide battery technology

papergraph.title: High-voltage LiCoO2 cathode
papergraph.abstract: High-voltage LiCoO2 suffers oxygen release, surface reconstruction, electrolyte oxidation and interfacial degradation. The mechanism of capacity fading remains unclear under high voltage cycling.
papergraph.limitation: Interfacial degradation and oxygen release remain unclear at high voltage.
papergraph.contribution: High-voltage LiCoO2 cathode High-voltage LiCoO2 suffers oxygen release, surface reconstruction, electrolyte oxidation and interfacial degradation.

knowledge_gaps.description: Source-grounded mechanism gap (interface degradation) for method=surface coating, scenario=high-voltage LiCoO2 cathode cycling, benchmark=capacity retention at 4.6 V: High-voltage LiCoO2 cathode High-voltage LiCoO2 suffers oxygen release, surface reconstruction, electrolyte oxidation and interfacial degradation.
knowledge_gaps.suggested_research_path: Isolate interfacial degradation pathways with surface/structure diagnostics and test protective modifications under accelerated stress.
knowledge_gaps.value_argument: This gap is grounded in a source-level mechanism/limitation/challenge statement, so it should outrank bare method-scenario matrix holes.
